source_pr.title: *: calculate GC safe point based on global min start timestamp
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
We do not need to increase GC life time or GC run interval to avoid the transaction aborted when it took too much time.

### What is changed and how it works?
1. calculate the new safe point using the minimum of (now - GC life time) and the global min transaction start timestamp.
1. remove the configurable max-txn-time-use and use a fixed 24 hours instead, which is the longest transaction life time.
1. remove the relation of GC life time and the max-txn-time-use.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
1. start 2 TiDB's cluster
1. connect to one TiDB, then begin; and get its start ts by information_schema.processlist
1. checkout /tidb/store/gcworker/saved_safe_point, it will pin to the min start ts
1. commit the transaction
1. checkout /tidb/store/gcworker/saved_safe_point again, and the safe point will update again.

Code changes

 - Has exported function/method change

Related changes

 - Need to update the documentation

symptoms: fix data race in test cases

fix_pattern: *: calculate GC safe point based on global min start timestamp
